name: Phenylephrine
executiveSummary: Pure alpha-adrenergic vasopressor; for intraoperative hypotension and anaphylaxis in selected regimens.
indications:
## Indications

- Hypotension during anesthesia.
- Vasopressor in anaphylaxis when indicated in the protocol.

## Precautions

- Reflex bradycardia. Use with caution in coronary artery disease.

> Adjust per institutional protocol and medical prescription.

adulto.presentation: Ampoule or vial for IV infusion according to institutional presentation.
adulto.reconstitution: Reconstitute according to package insert; dilute in 5% Dextrose or 0.9% NaCl.
adulto.finalConcentration: Concentration according to departmental chart (infusion pump).
adulto.dose: 50–100 mcg IV bolus or infusion 0.1–0.5 mcg/kg/min.
adulto.administration: Continuous IV via pump; central line preferred.
adulto.compatibility: Verify in-line compatibility with other vasopressors.
adulto.notes: Monitor heart rate, invasive blood pressure, urine output, and peripheral perfusion.

pediatrico.presentation: Ampoule or vial for IV infusion according to institutional presentation.
pediatrico.reconstitution: Reconstitute according to package insert; dilute in 5% Dextrose or 0.9% NaCl.
pediatrico.finalConcentration: Concentration according to departmental chart (infusion pump).
pediatrico.dose: 1–5 mcg/kg/dose bolus or infusion according to protocol.
pediatrico.infusionRate: Titrate according to blood pressure and perfusion.
pediatrico.administration: Continuous IV via pump; central line preferred.
pediatrico.compatibility: Verify in-line compatibility with other vasopressors.
pediatrico.notes: Monitor heart rate, invasive blood pressure, urine output, and peripheral perfusion.

neonatal.presentation: Ampoule or vial for IV infusion according to institutional presentation.
neonatal.reconstitution: Reconstitute according to package insert; dilute in 5% Dextrose or 0.9% NaCl.
neonatal.finalConcentration: Concentration according to departmental chart (infusion pump).
neonatal.dose: Bolus and infusion according to NICU chart; restricted use.
neonatal.infusionRate: Titrate according to blood pressure and perfusion.
neonatal.administration: Continuous IV via pump; central line preferred.
neonatal.compatibility: Verify in-line compatibility with other vasopressors.
neonatal.notes: Monitor heart rate, invasive blood pressure, urine output, and peripheral perfusion.

stability:
## Stability

- Use freshly prepared solution or according to package insert.
adverseEffects:
## Effects

- Reflex bradycardia, hypertension, headache, extravasation.

bibliography.citation: American Heart Association. ACLS / PALS / NRP Guidelines.
ANMAT. Drug information and authorized prescribing information in Argentina.
Society of Critical Care Medicine (SCCM). ICU medication guidelines.
American Academy of Pediatrics. Medication guidance in critical care.
